name: Vilalba – return via Puente Viejo and A Feira de Monte
name_original: Vilalba – vuelta por Puente Viejo y A Feira de Monte
description: Cycle 26.7 miles through Galicia's A Terra Chá region, passing rural villages, forests, and the historic Puente Viejo on this moderate route
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vilalba traverse the diverse landscapes of Galicia's Terra Chá region, characterized by rural charm, lush countryside, and extensive Galician farmland. The area offers varied terrain, including peaceful forests and verdant landscapes, with routes that range from mostly paved surfaces to some unpaved segments. While many routes feature gentle gradients, some paths include significant elevation gains. Vilalba also serves as a crossing point on the historic Northern Way of Saint James, providing scenic cycling opportunities. Best touring cycling routes around Vilalba The most popular touring cycling route is Vilalba – return via Puente Viejo and A Feira de Monte, a 26.7 miles (42.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside and historic river crossings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Alberte Bridge – Baamonde Church loop from Cemiterio de Insua, a difficult 32.1 miles (51.6 km) path. This route features varied terrain with notable elevation changes, passing through rural Galician landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Andrade Tower – Tower of Homage of Vilalba loop from Vilalba, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail leading through the region's historical landmarks and countryside, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Vilalba is defined by lush countryside, peaceful forests, and historic bridges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging long-distance treks. The routes in Vilalba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vilalba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Vilalba?
A: Vilalba offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, from leisurely rides on mostly paved surfaces to more challenging long-distance treks with significant elevation gains and some unpaved segments. The landscape features rural charm, lush countryside, and extensive Galician farmland, including peaceful forests and verdant areas. Many routes also incorporate historic river crossings and landmarks.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Vilalba?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For those seeking a moderate option that is still accessible, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017375399/vilalba-return-via-puente-viejo-and-a-feira-de-monte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilalba – return via Puente Viejo and A Feira de Monte&lt;/a&gt; is a 42.9 km trail that explores the local countryside and historic river crossings, suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: What are the more challenging touring cycling routes around Vilalba?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, Vilalba offers several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24566592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower – Tower of Homage of Vilalba loop from Vilalba&lt;/a&gt;, a 58.4 km trail that leads through historical landmarks and varied terrain. Another challenging ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Alberte Bridge – Baamonde Church loop from Cemiterio de Insua&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.6 km path featuring significant elevation changes through rural Galician landscapes.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Vilalba often pass by significant historical and natural attractions. You can encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient octagonal homage tower, and historic river crossings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Routes may also feature religious buildings like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4491959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Alberte Chapel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baamonde Church&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, you might pass by spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7570115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moss-Covered Stone Walls and Forest Path&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7586432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carballido Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop touring cycling routes available in Vilalba?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Vilalba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24566592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower – Tower of Homage of Vilalba loop from Vilalba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old bridge – San Alberte Bridge loop from A Igrexa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 75 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vilalba?
A: The region's lush countryside and verdant landscapes are particularly appealing during spring and early summer when the weather is generally mild and the flora is vibrant. Autumn also offers pleasant conditions with cooler temperatures and beautiful fall colors. While summer can be warm, the varied terrain and shaded forest paths can still provide enjoyable rides. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Vilalba?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vilalba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's rural charm, the peaceful forests, and the opportunity to explore historic bridges and landmarks. The diverse terrain, offering options for various ability levels, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific viewpoints on individual routes are not detailed, the region itself offers stunning panoramic views. A climb to the 935-meter summit of Mount Monseivane, for instance, provides magnificent vistas of Vilalba and its surrounding agricultural and livestock lands. Many routes traverse varied terrain that can offer scenic overlooks of the Galician countryside.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Vilalba varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017375399/vilalba-return-via-puente-viejo-and-a-feira-de-monte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilalba – return via Puente Viejo and A Feira de Monte&lt;/a&gt; (42.9 km) typically takes around 2 hours 41 minutes. More difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old bridge – Andrade Tower loop from Parrillada A Casilla&lt;/a&gt; (39.9 km), can take approximately 2 hours 55 minutes, while the 58.4 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24566592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower – Tower of Homage of Vilalba loop from Vilalba&lt;/a&gt; might take around 5 hours 3 minutes.

Q: Can I find places to relax or swim near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers natural spots for relaxation. The River Beach of La Magdalena (Playa Fluvial de la Magdalena) is an enchanting and tranquil spot with a large park perfect for walking along the O Muiño do Rañego River. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swimming in the Río de Parga&lt;/a&gt; highlight suggests opportunities for a refreshing dip. About 10 km from town, Charca do Alligal features a natural pool of mineral-medicinal thermal waters, surrounded by birch trees, offering a relaxing experience.

Q: Are there any routes that follow parts of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Vilalba serves as a significant crossing point on the historic Northern Way of Saint James (Camino del Norte). This provides cyclists with a scenic and less-crowded pilgrimage route that winds through peaceful forests and verdant landscapes, offering a unique cultural and spiritual dimension to your touring cycling experience.
tips: This tower with an octagonal base, an old homage tower, is the only vestige that reminds us of the existence of a fortress castle, whose origins date back to the 11th century, although this element dates from the 15th. Its silhouette dominates Vilalba, capital of the region known as Terra Chá. It is known that the old castle, successively remodeled from its origin, already had three towers and a polygonal wall in the 13th century. It belonged to the lords of Lemos, but later King Pedro I donated it to Fernán Pérez de Andrade or Boo, thus passing it to the heritage of this noble family, which will leave its heraldic symbols in the construction, such as the stone representation of the boar .
Laguna de Cospeito is a natural reserve and a protected habitat for migratory birds that come to rest and feed on the lake. Magic area to walk and add to your trip.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it offers picturesque rural roads and paths, some sections may include challenging climbs, earthy tracks, and even cobbled paths that could require dismounting and pushing your bike. It's suitable for cyclists with good to very good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Vilalba – return via Puente Viejo and A Feira de Monte route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, featuring a mix of rural roads, earthy tracks, and cement paths. You'll encounter both gentle riverside sections and more challenging hilly or mountainous climbs and descents. Some parts may even have cobbled surfaces, particularly near historical features.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several notable landmarks. You can see the historic Andrade Tower, cross an Old bridge, and enjoy views of the Laguna de Cospeito. The area is also rich in traditional Galician countryside, with small villages, historical churches, and natural forests.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and sections with challenging terrain, including potential cobbled paths and strong uphill sections, this route might be demanding for beginners or young children. Cyclists should have good fitness and some experience with varied terrain. There are often alternative asphalt routes available for the most challenging parts if you prefer a smoother ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route overlaps with sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers parts of the Northern Route (Galiza) and the Mtb Northern Route (Galiza). You'll also find smaller segments that coincide with the Ruta das Lagoas and the Sendeiro Histórico: Galicia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Vilalba area is generally during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the heat of summer and the potentially wetter, colder conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable cycling and better enjoyment of the rural landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, Vilalba, as a regional center, offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the town center or the main access points to the route, ensuring a convenient start and end to your ride.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point in Vilalba?
faqs.answer: Vilalba is a well-connected town in Lugo province. You can typically reach Vilalba by bus from larger cities in Galicia. Once in Vilalba, the starting point of the route should be accessible, though you may need to cycle a short distance from the bus station to the official trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in rural areas, specific regulations for this particular cycling route are not detailed. If cycling with a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, accustomed to bike travel, and always kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near livestock. Always carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It traverses public roads and trails through the Galician countryside, making it freely accessible for touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route or in Vilalba?
faqs.answer: Vilalba, being the starting and ending point, offers a good selection of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along the rural sections of the route, you might find smaller local establishments in villages, but it's always a good idea to carry snacks and water, especially on longer stretches, as services can be sparse.
